vocab.meaning: những năm tháng hình thành nhân cách/tư duy
vocab.example: I spent much of my formative years with a sketchbook in hand, exploring various shading techniques.
Even as a toddler, I exhibited a strong artistic inclination, constantly doodling on any available surface.

vocab.meaning: sự phối hợp giữa tay và mắt
vocab.example: Learning to paint at a young age is a fantastic way to develop precise hand-eye coordination.
In primary school, our teacher introduced us to basic color theory, teaching us how to mix primary hues.

vocab.meaning: vẽ vụng về/tô vẽ nguệch ngoạc (thường nói về trẻ con)
vocab.example: I remember happily daubing bright acrylics onto canvases without a care for the final result.

vocab.meaning: hình ảnh hóa các khái niệm
vocab.example: Drawing helped me learn how to visualize concepts, which eventually benefited my studies in other subjects like geometry.

vocab.meaning: sự sáng tạo không giới hạn/không bị kiềm chế
vocab.example: Childhood art sessions were all about unbridled creativity, before I became too self-critical of my work.
My parents enrolled me in an after-school program to nurture my talent for landscape painting.

vocab.meaning: khả năng nhận thức không gian
vocab.example: Sketching still-life objects greatly improved my spatial awareness and understanding of proportions.

vocab.meaning: cảm giác đạt được thành tựu
vocab.example: Finishing a complex watercolor piece gave me a massive sense of accomplishment when I was ten.
